### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

### Init Cursor

Shapes the item: writes `page`, `hasMore`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Page Loop

### Page Loop

Loop controller — releases 1 item(s) per pass. Output 2 (loop) processes the batch, output 1 (done) fires after the last batch.

Ports: ①→ Remove Duplicate Pages · ②→ Fetch Page from API

### Fetch Page from API

GET https://api.example.com/v1/pages — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Split Result Items · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Split Result Items

Explodes the `data` array into one item per element.

Next: Normalize Page Items

### Normalize Page Items

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming page records to a stable schema

Next: Page Loop

### Remove Duplicate Pages

Drops duplicates within this run (compared on `pageId`).

Next: Upsert Harvest into Google Sheets

### Upsert Harvest into Google Sheets

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Tally Harvest Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

### Tally Harvest Stats

Group-by + aggregate (like a pivot table) — turns raw rows into KPI numbers.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per page (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

discord — `action` operation against the connected account.

# Track site health with HTTP / REST API and Google Sheets at scale

**Pattern:** SEO Audit · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Track site health with HTTP / REST API and Google Sheets at scale. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual rank tracking is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Init Cursor → Page Loop → Remove Duplicate Pages → Upsert Harvest into Google Sheets → Tally Harvest Stats → Serialize Run Output (CSV) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to seo audit without manual effort
- Anyone automating rank tracking in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your rank tracking process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
